text:
私の名前はKevin Tadejです。風景写真家であり、長時間露光写真を専門としています。
私はフランスのフォトフェスティバルで頻繁に作品を展示しており、写真専門の旅行代理店であるWorldway Photo（パリ）で現役の講師を務めています。私は、写真と絵画、特に私の「Mer Nature」シリーズのような水彩画を融合させることに強い情熱を注いでいます。そのためには特定の機材が必要であり、ここからは私のテクニックについて詳しくお話しします。
画像を制作するためには、フィルターとフィルターホルダーシステムが必要です。数年前、私は1970年代に写真フィルターの創始者となったCokin®製の樹脂フィルターから使い始めました。こうしたフィルターのおかげで、質の高い風景写真を撮ることができ、特にGND8フィルターを使用して空を暗くすることができました。しかし、すぐにこれらの樹脂フィルターにいくつかの問題に直面しました。第一に、非常に狭いフォトバッグに収納していた樹脂フィルターが曲がったり歪んだりする傾向がありました。第二に、海岸環境での長時間露光のエキスパートであるため、強風や、何よりもあらゆる海景写真家の悩みの種である砂といった過酷な気象条件に直面します。実際、風と砂が徐々に樹脂フィルターを傷つけ、あっという間に破損させてしまいました。最後に、樹脂フィルターを重ねて使用すると色差が生じ、マゼンタやブルーへの色被りが発生することがあります。
現在はCokin Nuances Extremeシリーズのフィルターに切り替えました。これらのフィルターが樹脂フィルターとは全く別物であることはすぐに分かります。光学的な品質と堅牢性の面で大きな進歩を遂げています。今では色被りを起こさずにフィルターを重ねることができます。さらに、Nuances Extremeフィルターは、困難な気象条件下で活動する写真家のために作られています。実際、時には非常に困難な様々な状況で使用しましたが、操作上の問題は全くありませんでした。
2019年9月、私は2つのフランスのフォトフェスティバルの合間に、フランス東部の低山地帯であるヴォージュ山脈を旅しました。私の目的は、新しい長時間露光写真のシリーズに取り組み、過酷な気象条件がもたらす雰囲気を捉えることでした。初日、ドノン山の山頂に到達した際、フィルターを使用してこれからやってくる雨のカーテンを予測することができました。ND1024とGND8フィルターを使い、1分間の露光でこの美しい雨のカーテンを撮影することに成功しました。
Kevin Tadej, Vosges, top of Donon, Cokin Nuances Extreme ND1024 and GND8S, September 2019.
雲と雨と風の中で2時間足止めされた後、ようやく素晴らしい夕焼けが現れました。これについては記事の最後で詳しくお話しします。わずか4時間の短い睡眠の後、私はオネック山の山頂までハイキングに行きました。天気予報は楽観的でしたが、大自然に騙されました……。強風と激しい雨に見舞われ、本当に寒くなってきました。迷った後、私はモチベーションを高めてヴォージュの空気を追い求め、同時にこの短い遠征から他の画像も捉えようと試みました。
Kevin Tadej, Vosges, top of Hohneck, Cokin Nuances Extreme ND1024 and GND8S, September 2019.
この風景には、センサーに届く光量を減らして露光時間を延ばすためにND1024フィルターを、そして雲を暗くするためにGND8フィルターを使用しました。ある日の日の出の際、ND1024、GND8、そして偏光フィルターを使用して以下の画像を撮影することができました。この風景を制作する際、露光時間を長くするためにND1024フィルターを、雲を暗くするためにGND8フィルターを使用しました。偏光フィルターに関しては、コントラストと彩度を高めることで雲を際立たせるために使用しました。
Kevin Tadej, Vosges, Cokin Nuances Extrême ND1024 and GND8S, EVO CPL, September 2019.
リバースグラデーション R-GNDフィルター
また、私のフォトバッグにはNuances ExtremeのリバースグラデーションNDフィルターも用意していました。これまでこのR-GNDフィルターを使ったことはありませんでしたが、嬉しいことに、特定の撮影条件下でのそのフルポテンシャルを発見しました。これらのフィルターは、下部付近がより濃くなっており……
translation:
My name is Kevin Tadej. I am a landscape photographer specializing in long-exposure photography.
I frequently exhibit my work at French photo festivals and currently work as an instructor at Worldway Photo (Paris), a photography travel agency. I have a strong passion for merging photography and painting, especially watercolors like my "Mer Nature" series. This requires specific equipment, so I will talk more about my techniques from here.
To create images, I need a filter and a filter holder system. Several years ago, I started with resin filters made by Cokin®, who pioneered photographic filters in the 1970s. These filters allowed me to take high-quality landscape photos, and I was able to darken the sky, especially using the GND8 filter. However, I soon encountered several problems with these resin filters. First, the resin filters, which I stored in a very narrow photo bag, tended to bend or warp. Second, as an expert in long-exposure in coastal environments, I face harsh weather conditions such as strong winds and, above all, sand, which is a common problem for all seascape photographers. In fact, the wind and sand gradually damaged the resin filters, causing them to break in no time. Finally, when stacking resin filters, color shifts occurred, sometimes resulting in magenta or blue color casts.
I have now switched to the Cokin Nuances Extreme series of filters. It is immediately clear that these filters are completely different from resin filters. They represent a huge leap forward in optical quality and durability. I can now stack filters without color casts. Furthermore, the Nuances Extreme filters are designed for photographers working in difficult weather conditions. In fact, I used them in various very challenging situations, and there were absolutely no operational issues.
In September 2019, between two French photo festivals, I traveled through the Vosges Mountains in eastern France. My goal was to work on a new series of long-exposure photographs, capturing the atmosphere brought by harsh weather conditions. On the first day, upon reaching the summit of Mont Donon, I was able to predict the curtain of rain that was coming using filters. I successfully captured this beautiful curtain of rain with a 1-minute exposure using ND1024 and GND8 filters.
Kevin Tadej, Vosges, top of Donon, Cokin Nuances Extreme ND1024 and GND8S, September 2019.
After being stranded for two hours in clouds, rain, and wind, a magnificent sunset finally appeared. I will talk more about this at the end of the article. After only four hours of short sleep, I hiked to the summit of Hohneck. The weather forecast was optimistic, but nature tricked me... I was hit by strong winds and heavy rain, and it got really cold. After hesitating, I motivated myself to chase the air of the Vosges, while also trying to capture other images from this short expedition.
Kevin Tadej, Vosges, top of Hohneck, Cokin Nuances Extreme ND1024 and GND8S, September 2019.
For this landscape, I used the ND1024 filter to reduce the amount of light reaching the sensor and extend the exposure time, and the GND8 filter to darken the clouds. On a sunrise one day, I was able to take the following image using ND1024, GND8, and a polarizing filter. When creating this landscape, I used the ND1024 filter to lengthen the exposure time and the GND8 filter to darken the clouds. I used the polarizing filter to make the clouds stand out by increasing contrast and saturation.
Kevin Tadej, Vosges, Cokin Nuances Extrême ND1024 and GND8S, EVO CPL, September 2019.
Reverse Gradient R-GND Filter
I also had Nuances Extreme Reverse Gradient ND filters in my photo bag. I had never used these R-GND filters before, but happily, I discovered their full potential under specific shooting conditions. These filters are darker towards the bottom...

text:
全か無かではない：抗ウイルス・ロックダウンは緩やかに解除される可能性がある

コロナウイルスの拡散を抑えるための何らかのロックダウン下で生活している数百万人ものアメリカ人にとって、制限がいつ終わるのか分からないことは大きな不安の種となっている。結婚式や葬式、あるいは単に友人との夜の外出といった人生のイベントは、数週間、数ヶ月、あるいはもっと長く延期されるのだろうか。

ドナルド・トランプ大統領は今週、制限が経済に与えた深刻なダメージを挙げ、4月12日のイースターまでには企業が再開することを望むと答えを出した。しかし、ほとんどの公衆衛生の専門家は、COVID-19の感染者がピークに達し、減少に転じる前に制限を解除することは無謀であり、経済に同様のダメージを与えかねない第2波を解き放つことになると警告している。

科学者たちは、いつ制限を安全に緩和できるかを正確に予測することに消極的だが、この新ウイルスに最初に襲われた中国で観察されたことに基づけば、ロックダウン実施から約6〜8週間後にいくらかの緩和が訪れる可能性がある。これは、ロックダウン開始から2〜3週間後に症例がピークに達し、その後2〜3週間かけて徐々に減少するという仮定に基づいている。

生活がいつ正常に戻るかという明確なタイムラインを切望するかもしれないが、科学者たちは、それは正確には正しい問いではないと言う。日常が以前と全く同じように再開されるまでには数ヶ月、あるいはそれ以上の時間がかかるだろう。しかし、それはそれまで全員が完全なロックダウンに閉じ込められていることを意味するわけではない。

「COVID-19のワクチンが開発されるまで、単に2年間家の中で待っているわけにはいかない」と、コロンビア大学の疾患研究者であるスティーブン・モースは述べた。「私たちは何らかの方法で普通の生活に戻る道を見つけなければならない」

制限をどのように、いつ解除するかという決定は、検査を増やすことでしか得られない感染率に関する情報に基づいたものであるべきだと彼は付け加えた。そうすることで、政策立案者は地域ごとのアウトブレイクに合わせて制限を調整できるようになる。

ロックダウンを、国を完全に閉鎖するか、あるいはすべてのルールを窓から投げ捨てるかという二択のオン/オフスイッチのように考えるのではなく、多くの科学者は、制限を強めたり弱めたりできる中間的なコースを計画することを提唱している。ロックダウンに、回して上げ下げできるボリュームダイヤルがあると考えてほしい。

「より多くの情報があれば、最も効果的な対応をターゲットに絞ることができる」と、ボストン大学の疾患研究者であるナディア・アブエレザムは述べた。それは、段階的に人々を労働力に戻していくことを意味するかもしれない、と彼女は提案した。

科学者たちは、コロナウイルスに先に襲われた他の国々で何が起きているかを注視し、それらの場所が制限を緩和したときにどうなるかを見守っている。

多くの中国の都市では、国がロックダウンを開始してから約2ヶ月後、レストランやオフィスが再開し始めた。エレベーターや会議室に入れる人数に制限があるなど、依然として制限は設けられており、広範な検査も継続されている。

第2のアウトブレイクが起きれば、将来的に再び締め付けが行われる可能性がある。

「人々は、しばらくの間は完全に普通の生活に戻ることはないという事実に備えるべきだ」と、ロンドン大学衛生熱帯医学大学院の疾患研究者であるマーク・ジットは述べた。「しかし同時に、トンネルの先に光が見えることを人々に見せる必要もある」

制限という比喩的なダイヤルは、拡大された検査によって、ある地域で現在何人が感染しているか、そして過去の感染から何人が回復したかが明らかになるにつれ、異なる位置に設定されることになるだろう。

目標は、新規感染の可能性を制限しつつ、人々が部分的に日常のルーチンに戻れるようにすることだ。

新しいコロナウイルスは多くの人に軽度または中程度の症状を引き起こすが、入院を必要とするほど重症なケースがわずか（14%から20%の間）であったとしても、流行の規模があまりに大きいため、病院や医療従事者、そして無関係な処置の延期を余儀なくされる可能性のある他の患者に多大な負担を強いることになる。

木曜日、残酷な節目となる出来事が起き、t（以下、原文が途切れているためそのまま）ということを思い出させた。
translation:
Not All or Nothing: Viral Lockdowns May Ease Gradually

For millions of Americans living under some form of lockdown to curb the spread of the coronavirus, the uncertainty of when restrictions will end is a major source of anxiety. Will life events like weddings, funerals, or simply going out with friends be postponed for weeks, months, or even longer?

President Donald Trump responded this week by citing the severe damage restrictions have caused to the economy and expressing a hope that businesses will reopen by Easter on April 12th. However, most public health experts warn that lifting restrictions before COVID-19 cases peak and begin to decline would be reckless, potentially unleashing a second wave that could inflict similar damage on the economy.

While scientists are hesitant to predict exactly when restrictions can be safely eased, based on what was observed in China, the first country hit by this new virus, some easing might occur about 6 to 8 weeks after the lockdown is implemented. This is based on the assumption that cases will peak 2 to 3 weeks after the lockdown begins and then gradually decline over the following 2 to 3 weeks.

While people may long for a clear timeline for when life will return to normal, scientists say that is not precisely the right question. It will likely take months, or even longer, before daily life resumes exactly as it was before. However, that does not mean everyone will be confined to a complete lockdown until then.

"We can't just wait at home for two years until the COVID-19 vaccine is developed," said Steven Morse, a disease researcher at Columbia University. "We have to find a way to return to a semblance of normal life."

He added that the decision of how and when to lift restrictions should be based on infection rate data obtained by increasing testing. This would allow policymakers to adjust restrictions according to regional outbreaks.

Instead of viewing lockdowns as a binary on/off switch—either completely shutting down the country or throwing all rules out the window—many scientists advocate for planning an intermediate course where restrictions can be tightened or loosened. Think of a volume dial for the lockdown that can be turned up or down.

"With more information, we can target the most effective response," said Nadia Abelesam, a disease researcher at Boston University. She suggested this might mean gradually bringing people back to the workforce.

Scientists are closely watching what is happening in other countries that were hit by the coronavirus first, observing what happens when those places ease restrictions.

In many Chinese cities, restaurants and offices began to reopen about two months after the country initiated the lockdown. Restrictions remain in place, such as limits on the number of people allowed in elevators and meeting rooms, and extensive testing continues.

If a second outbreak occurs, further tightening may happen in the future.

"People should prepare for the fact that we won't return to completely normal life for a while," said Mark Zitt, a disease researcher at the London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine. "But at the same time, we need to show people there is light at the end of the tunnel."

The metaphorical dial of restrictions will be set at different positions as expanded testing reveals how many people are currently infected in a given area and how many have recovered from past infections.

The goal is to limit the possibility of new infections while allowing people to return to partial daily routines.

While the new coronavirus causes mild or moderate symptoms for many, even if the severe cases requiring hospitalization are only a small percentage (between 14% and 20%), the sheer scale of the epidemic places a huge burden on hospitals, healthcare workers, and other patients whose non-essential procedures must be postponed.

On Thursday, a cruel milestone occurred, reminding us of t (the original text is cut off here).

text:
この記事およびその目的において、我々が話しているのは、自宅やガソリンスタンドの駐車場で使用される護身用ハンドガンについてだ。狩猟用ハンドガンや、法執行機関が携帯するような「公務用」ピストルの話をしているのではない。隠し持つのに買ったか、あるいは自分や他人を守るためにすぐに手に取れるようナイトスタンドに置いたもののことを指している。続きは「ピストルのアップグレード提案 パート1」へ→

通常、それらは次弾の給弾不良（スライドが閉じる前に次弾を押し上げるバネ圧が不足しているか、フォロワーが固着している）、ストーブパイプ、またはダブルフィード（新弾が跳ね上がり、撃ち終わったばかりの空薬莢に引っかかって、それをチャンバーに押し戻す）を引き起こす。これらの問題には他の原因もあり得るし、これが不良マガジンが引き起こすことのすべてであるとは限らない。

取り外し可能なマガジンを備えた銃を所有しているなら、マガジンをたくさん持つべきだ。それらは交換可能な部品であり、性質上、摩耗するものだ。2つしか手元に置いていないというのは賢い考えではないし、私の読者は平均的なクマよりも賢い。これはプレッパーであることや恐怖心とは関係なく、知能の問題だ。

賢い人間は価格が低い時に買う。今、価格はCOVID前、つまりブッシュ・ジュニア大統領の時代以来、銃器関連のあらゆるものの価格が最低だったトランプ政権時代の水準まで戻っている。

マイクロビジネスとして、私は利益を出すことに賛成だ。しかし、企業が顧客を利用する時があり、それは正しくない。9/11のテロの際、私たちの地域ではいくつかのガソリンスタンドがガソリン価格を吊り上げた。ミズーリ州司法長官が行動を起こし、価格は引き下げられ、それらの特定のスタンドは一定期間、誰よりも安く燃料を販売することを強制された。続きは「Dirtより安い？そんなわけないだろ！」へ→

更新：更新内容については、記事の最後へ進んでくれ。

「フロントサイトに集中しろ」。トレーニングを受けたことがあるか、まともな射手に助けてもらったことがあるなら、きっとこれを聞いたことがあるだろう。

過去60年間のあらゆるインストラクターによれば、標的に当てるためにはフロントサイトに集中しなければならない。そこに集中するのは「合理的」に思える。リアサイトと標的を一直線に保ち、フロントサイトを使うことで、狙っている標的により正確に当てることができる。私が抱いている疑問は、フロントサイトに集中している時に、どうやって標的の一点に狙いを定めるのかということだ。続きは（2020/11/15更新）「ピストルのレッドドット/リフレックスサイト vs 伝統的なサイト」へ→

2018年3月、友人のマークが私のもうすぐ来る誕生日にホルスターを贈りたいと言った。彼はLenwoodのサイトに行って、自分が欲しいものを正確にデザインしろと言ったので、私はそうした。

私はGlock 34 MOS用のホルスターが欲しかった。選んだのはHighlanderパターンのWraithだ。Wraithはウェストバンド外携行（OWB）用に設計されている。続きは「Lenwoodホルスターレビュー」へ→

以前の記事で、私を大切に扱ってくれたArmaliteとTrijiconに感謝の手紙を書いた。Armaliteがどれほど良くしてくれたかはここで、Trijiconについてはここで読める。それを踏まえ、私はEOTechが困難な時期にある時も忠誠を誓い続けた。

リフレックスサイトやレッドドットサイトは、長年さまざまな方法でピストルに装着されてきた。どれも安くはなかった。GlockがMOS（Miniature Optic System）のアイデアを導入したとき、私は即座にいくつかの利点に気づいた。銃にシステムを組み込むことで、かさばる追加マウントや、保証を無効にし、かつ一つのブランドの光学機器にしか適合しない高価なスライドカットが不要になる。私は遠近両用眼鏡をかけているが、リフレックスサイトなら標的に集中すれば「レッドドット」も同時にピントが合う。これで標的捕捉速度が大幅に上がることを期待していた。続きは「Glock 19 MOS もう一つの人生の冒険」へ→
translation:
In this article and for its purpose, we are talking about defensive handguns used at home or in gas station parking lots. We are not talking about hunting handguns or "duty" pistols carried by law enforcement. This refers to something you bought to keep hidden, or something you placed on a nightstand so you could grab it quickly to protect yourself or others. Continue to "Pistol Upgrade Suggestions Part 1" →

Typically, these cause feeding issues with the next round (insufficient spring pressure to push the next round up before the slide closes, or a stuck follower), stovepipes, or double feeds (a new round bounces up and gets caught on the just-fired empty casing, pushing it back into the chamber). There can be other causes for these problems, and this is not everything that a faulty magazine can cause.

If you own a gun with detachable magazines, you should have plenty of them. They are interchangeable parts and are subject to wear. Having only two on hand is not a smart idea, and my readers are smarter than the average bear. This is a matter of intelligence, not being a prepper or being fearful.

Smart people buy when prices are low. Right now, prices for everything gun-related have returned to the levels of the Trump administration, which was the lowest since before COVID, that is, during the Bush Jr. administration.

As a micro-business, I am fine with making a profit. However, there are times when corporations take advantage of customers, and that is wrong. During the 9/11 terrorist attacks, several gas stations in our area inflated gas prices. The Missouri Attorney General took action, prices were lowered, and those specific stations were forced to sell fuel cheaper than anyone else for a period of time. Continue to "Cheaper Than Dirt? No Way!" →

Update: For update details, proceed to the end of the article.

"Focus on the front sight." If you have ever been trained or helped by a competent shooter, you have probably heard this.

According to every instructor over the past 60 years, you must focus on the front sight to hit the target. It seems "reasonable" to focus there. By keeping the rear sight and the target in line and using the front sight, you can hit the intended target more accurately. My question is, when focusing on the front sight, how do you aim at a single point on the target? Continue to (Updated 11/15/2020) "Pistol Red Dot/Reflex Sight vs. Traditional Sights" →

In March 2018, my friend Mark said he wanted to give me a holster for my upcoming birthday. He went to Lenwood Sights and told me to design exactly what I wanted.

I wanted a holster for the Glock 34 MOS. I chose the Wraith in the Highlander pattern. The Wraith is designed for open-carry (OWB). Continue to "Lenwood Holster Review" →

In a previous article, I wrote a letter of thanks to Armalite and Trijicon for treating me well. You can read about how well Armalite treated me here, and about Trijicon here. Based on that, I continued to remain loyal even when EOTech was going through difficult times.

Reflex and red dot sights have been mounted on pistols in various ways for many years. None of them were cheap. When Glock introduced the MOS (Miniature Optic System) idea, I immediately noticed several advantages. By integrating the system into the gun, you eliminate bulky extra mounts, expensive slide cuts that only fit one brand of optics, and voiding the warranty. I wear bifocals, but with a reflex sight, the "red dot" comes into focus simultaneously when you focus on the target. I expected this to significantly increase target acquisition speed. Continue to "Glock 19 MOS Another Life Adventure" →

text:
Alexaに新しいツールバー作成機能が追加され、I Programmer Alexaツールバーをダウンロードして、私たちの新しい記事をチェックできるようになりました。しかし、誰もがツールバーを好むわけではありません。

ツールバーやブラウザのアドイン――大嫌いじゃないですか？それはクッキーのようなものです。見知らぬ人からクッキーを受け取るという概念が集合意識に浸透するまでは、無害な状態変数に過ぎませんでした。アドインやツールバーも同様に、ブラウザをクラッシュさせるために設計された仕掛けか、あるいはあなたを監視するための何かなのです。

Alexaツールバーはあなたを監視しており、それを隠そうともしません。もちろん匿名ではありますが、あなたが訪れたウェブサイトを記録し、ウェブ上のトラフィックフローの全体像を構築します。ビッグデータの時代に、ウェブサイトの人気の決定的な概要が一つもないというのは衝撃的です。もちろん、欠陥だらけのAlexaシステムを除いては。トラフィックを測定する他の方法は、一般的にサイト側がオプトインし、さらにはその特権に金を払うことさえ求められます。

自分の閲覧習慣をAlexaに提供するためにすべきことは、標準のAlexaツールバーか、I Programmer's Toolbarのようなカスタマイズされたツールバーのいずれかをインストールすることだけです。

なぜAlexaツールバーの作成と、それを推奨する説明までこんなに時間がかかったのかと思うかもしれません。その理由は、Amazonがウェブ分析の実験を終了させようとしているという噂があり、ここ数年Alexaの将来が少し不透明に見えたからです。しかし、Alexaには新たな生命の兆しがあります。ツールバーは現在、IE9、Chrome、Firefoxという3つのモダンブラウザで動作しており、Alexaのサイト上にも刷新の兆候が見られます。ですから、公的なウェブメトリクスを持つために、閲覧習慣を寄付するという問題を提起するには、今が良いタイミングだと思われます。

これは完璧な解決策ではありません。Alexaツールバーをインストールし、データに寄与するユーザー層には偏りがあります。したがって、統計データは比較的簡単に偽装できてしまいます。また、プレミアムサービスに加入しない限り、Alexaは収集したすべてのデータを提供しません。しかし、最後の欠点を除けば、より多くの人がAlexaツールバーを使用することで改善可能ですし、公開されている統計だけでも、ウェブサイトが全体の構図の中でどこに位置しているかを合理的に把握するには十分です。

もしあなたがビッグデータと測定を信じるなら、Alexaツールバーをインストールすることは、おそらくあなたの義務でしょう。一方で、自分の閲覧習慣でデータを歪ませたくないと思うかもしれませんね！
translation:
A new toolbar creation feature has been added to Alexa, allowing you to download the I Programmer Alexa Toolbar and check out our new article. However, not everyone likes toolbars.

Toolbars and browser add-ons—don't you hate them? They are like cookies. Until the concept of receiving cookies from strangers permeated the collective consciousness, they were merely harmless state variables. Add-ons and toolbars are similarly either mechanisms designed to crash your browser or something to monitor you.

The Alexa Toolbar monitors you, and it doesn't try to hide it. While it is anonymous, it records the websites you visit and builds a complete picture of the web traffic flow. In the age of big data, it is shocking that there is no definitive overview of website popularity. Of course, not excluding the flawed Alexa system. Other methods of measuring traffic generally require the site itself to opt-in, and sometimes even pay for that privilege.

All you have to do to provide your browsing habits to Alexa is install either the standard Alexa Toolbar or a customized toolbar like I Programmer's Toolbar.

You might wonder why it took so long to create the Alexa Toolbar and even to provide an explanation recommending it. The reason is that there were rumors that Amazon was trying to end its web analytics experiments, and the future of Alexa had looked somewhat uncertain in recent years. However, there are signs of new life for Alexa. The toolbar currently works on three modern browsers: IE9, Chrome, and Firefox, and there are signs of renewal on Alexa's site as well. Therefore, I think now is a good time to raise the issue of donating browsing habits to have public web metrics.

This is not a perfect solution. There is a bias in the user base that installs the Alexa Toolbar and contributes data. Therefore, the statistical data can be relatively easily falsified. Also, Alexa does not provide all the collected data unless you subscribe to a premium service. But apart from that last drawback, it can be improved by more people using the Alexa Toolbar, and even the publicly available statistics are sufficient to reasonably grasp where a website is positioned within the overall landscape.

If you believe in big data and measurement, installing the Alexa Toolbar is probably your duty. On the other hand, you might not want to distort data with your browsing habits!

text:
ライト！カメラ！殺人！ミステリー・ワインディナー
8月9日（金）
イベントについて
ルビーのような血塗られたレッドカーペットの上で、どんでん返しと混乱、そして恐怖に満ちた、ティンセルタウンでの刺激的な一夜を！ハリウッドの死んだAリスト芸能人たちと社交に耽りましょう。秘密が暴かれ、キャリアが築かれ、スキャンダルが弾ける……そして、殺人が起こります！あなたのお気に入りのセレブや映画のキャラクター（生存・死亡問わず！）になりきって、最高にキメた格好で来てください。受賞歴のあるMessina Hofのワインと共に、グルメディナーをご堪能ください！
マーダーミステリー・ディナーの内容
Messina Hofは、中世まで遡る独自の「ディナーシアター」であるマーダーミステリーでよく知られています。かつての客は、食事をしながら俳優や語り手によるエンターテインメントを楽しみました。しかし、Messina Hofのマーダーミステリーでは、ゲストの皆様こそが俳優であり、語り手となります。イベントの日までに、各ゲストにキャラクターの割り当てが届きますので、それに合わせて衣装を決めてください。
各マーダーミステリー・ディナーには、同様にテーマに沿ったホストがつき、いわば「舞台を整え」、物語へと導いてくれます。素晴らしい3コースのディナーが提供されるだけでなく、各コースにはMessina Hofの受賞ワインが専門的にペアリングされています。コースの合間に、各キャラクターは交流し、誰が犯人なのかの手がかりを集めることができます。あるいは、犯人はあなたでしょうか？もしそうなら、疑われないよう十分に賢く立ち回ってください……。
ブラッドオレンジとザクロのサラダ
スパイシーなルッコラに、オレンジのシュプレーム、ザクロ、ローストしたロイヤルピーカンナッツ、パルメザンチーズを合わせ、シェリーバルサミコ・ヴィネグレットソースで和えました。
ニューヨークストリップステーキのブラックンド
44 Farm産のストリップステーキにブラックニングスパイスをまぶして炭火焼きにし、ソテーしたアスパラガスとブルーカニを添えたシャルドネ・オスカーソースをたっぷりとかけました。
チョコレートチーズケーキ
グラハムクラッカーの土台に、リースリング・ラズベリークーリを添えて。
「ライト！カメラ！殺人！」のチケットにはすべて食事とワインが含まれています。コスチューム着用を推奨します！！チケットはすべてのMessina Hofワイナリー店舗、またはオンラインで購入可能です。「チケット購入」ボタンをクリックして、今すぐチケットを手に入れてください！
- 一般入場券 | 69.95ドル（税・チップ別）
一般入場およびワイン付きの3コース食事が含まれます。
ヒルカントリー店で開催されるハーベストフェスティバルへの参加にご興味がありますか？
詳細はこちらをクリックしてください。
詳細については、お電話 (979) 778-9463 内線234
またはメール firstname.lastname@example.org までお問い合わせください。
translation:
Lights! Camera! Murder! Mystery Wine Dinner
Friday, August 9th
About the Event
Join us for a thrilling night in Tinsel Town, filled with twists, chaos, and terror, on a ruby-red, blood-soaked red carpet! Socialize with Hollywood's deceased A-list celebrities. Secrets will be revealed, careers will be built, scandals will explode... and murder will occur! Come dressed in your most fabulous attire as your favorite celebrity or movie character (living or dead!). Indulge in a gourmet dinner paired with award-winning wines from Messina Hof!
About the Murder Mystery Dinner
Messina Hof is well-known for its murder mystery dinners, a unique "dinner theater" experience dating back to the Middle Ages. Past guests enjoyed entertainment provided by actors and narrators while dining. However, in Messina Hof's murder mystery, you, the guests, become the actors and narrators. Character assignments will be sent to each guest before the event, so please plan your costumes accordingly.
Each murder mystery dinner will have a host, similarly themed, who will "set the stage" and guide you into the story. Not only will a wonderful three-course dinner be served, but each course will be professionally paired with award-winning wines from Messina Hof. Between courses, characters will interact and gather clues as to who the killer is. Or perhaps, are you the killer? If so, you'll need to play your part cleverly enough to avoid suspicion...
Blood Orange and Pomegranate Salad
Mixed with spicy arugula, orange supremes, pomegranate, roasted royal pecans, and Parmesan cheese, dressed with a sherry balsamic vinaigrette.
Blackened New York Strip Steak
44 Farm strip steak coated in blackening spice and grilled over charcoal, served with a generous pour of Chardonnay Oscar sauce over sautéed asparagus and blue crab.
Chocolate Cheesecake
On a graham cracker crust, served with a Riesling raspberry coulis.
Tickets for "Lights! Camera! Murder!" include all meals and wine. Costume attire is encouraged!! Tickets are available for purchase at all Messina Hof vineyards or online. Click the "Buy Tickets" button to get yours now!
- General Admission | $69.95 (plus tax and tip)
Includes general admission and a three-course meal with wine.
Interested in attending the Harvest Festival at Hill Country?
Click here for details.
For more information, please call (979) 778-9463 ext. 234
or email firstname.lastname@example.org.

text:
来週木曜の午後4時という時間が、まるで恐ろしい重圧のようにのしかかっている。昔の漫画で、いつ落ちてくるかわからない金敷が頭上にぶら下がっているような感じだ。なぜ自分に就業不能手当が必要なのか、どれほど病んでいるのかを正当化し、弁明しなければならないのはひどい話だし、そんなことをさせられるのが大嫌いだ。それに伴う「駆け引き」や、彼らのガイドラインに合わせるために真実を曲げなければならない必要性も忌々しい。毎日、来る日も来る日もどれほど疲れているかといった、仕事をする能力に本当に影響している事柄を全く考慮しない、あのシステムの硬直性が憎い。私が切望している多くのことを妨げているのはこの「倦怠感」なのだが、助けようとしている（あるいは、彼らの役割がそうであるように、妨げようとしている……）専門家にこれを伝えるのに苦労している。最近では、どんな仕事がしたいのか、勉強を続けるつもりはあるのか、就労関連の活動やボランティアはどうするのか、などと繰り返し聞かれる。だが、今の私には髪を切る気力も、友人に会う気力も、教会グループに行く気力さえもない。そんな状態で、どうやって他のあらゆることをやる気力を出せというのか。

すべては神の手にあると分かっているから、神を信頼しようとしているが、苦しんでいる。この手当が神による備えなのか、それとも神が別の計画を持っているのかさえ分からない。手当の不服申し立てで不安なもう一つの点は、真実についてだ。何を言うべきか指示されたが、その一部は100%真実ではないし、真実を曖昧にしたり、都合よく省略したりしている。ある程度、これは仕方ない面もある。30分テレビを見て内容を理解できれば「就労可能」と判断されるようなシステムでは、時として言うことに注意しなければならない。また、最悪に調子が悪い日がどのようなものかを思い出し、それについて話す必要があるが、私はそれが非常に苦手だ。自分の気分がどれほどひどいかを控えめに言うことに慣れているので、これは非常に直感に反することだ。しかし、イエスの信徒として正直でありたいので、真っ赤な嘘はつきたくない。

昨日このことについて考えていた後、スパージョンの『信仰の小切手帳』のその日の項目を読んだところ、言葉がページから飛び出して私に語りかけてくるようだった。

「読者は、嘘や疑わしい行為によって困難から脱しようと、一瞬たりとも試みてはならない。むしろ、真実と誠実という大通りの真ん中を歩き続けなさい。それが最善の道である。人生において、遠回りをしたり、ごまかしを考えたりしてはならない。公正であり、恐れるな。イエスに従い、悪い結果など気にするな。もし最悪の不幸を悪行によって避けられたとしても、その試み自体が、他のどんな不幸よりもひどい悪に陥ることになるだろう。神の道こそが最善の道であるはずだ。人々から愚かだと思われようともそれに従いなさい。そうすれば、真に賢くなるだろう」

だから、祈り、信頼しようと努め、完全に精神崩壊しないようにしながら、最善を尽くして準備している。これらの手当の手続きは私のメンタルヘルスに非常に悪影響を及ぼしており、それが腹立たしい。というのも、認知行動療法（CBT）でようやく前進していると感じ始めていたし、自分に対して前向きになり、ようやくうつ病から回復しつつあると感じて喜んでいたところだったからだ。

この不服申し立てに勝ちたいのかどうか分からない。勝てばいくらかのお金が入るし、それは歓迎しないことではないが、同時にあらゆる「就労への道」の面接や追加の要求が復活することになる。さらに、障害生活手当（DLA）を含むより多くの手当を申請すべきだという話まで出ており、考えるだけで耐えられない。人生に価値がないように感じてしまう。一体何のためにこんなことをしているのか。ひどい体調に加え、この凄まじいストレスのせいで、人生がとても魅力のないものに見える。これはメンタルヘルスにとって最悪だ。DWP（就業年金省）は、私を価値のない人間だと思わせ、罪悪感を抱かせ、役に立たない人間だと思わせ、また様々なレベルで（例えば、編み物ができ、たくさん編んでいることなどで）詐欺師であるかのように感じさせる。このシステムが憎い。打ちのめされている時にさらに蹴られるような気分だ。

こんなことで大騒ぎしている自分は、本当に意気地なしだと思う。
translation:
The time of 4 PM next Thursday feels like a terrifying weight pressing down on me. It's like the golden weights from old manga are hanging above my head, unsure when they will drop. It's awful that I have to justify and defend how sick I am and why I need sickness benefits, and I hate being made to do that. I also detest the "bargaining" that comes with it, and the necessity of twisting the truth to fit their guidelines. I hate the rigidity of that system, which completely fails to consider things that genuinely affect my ability to work, like how exhausted I am day after day. This "malaise" is what is preventing me from achieving so much I desire, but I struggle to convey this to the professionals who are trying to help (or, as their role seems to be, hindering me...). Recently, they keep asking me things like what kind of work I want to do, if I plan to keep studying, and what I'll do about work-related activities or volunteering. But right now, I don't even have the energy to cut my hair, or the energy to meet friends, or even the energy to go to a church group. How am I supposed to muster the energy for anything else in that state?

I know everything is in God's hands, so I am trying to trust God, but I am suffering. I don't even know if this benefit is God's provision or if God has a different plan. Another source of anxiety regarding the benefit appeal is the truth. I've been instructed on what to say, but some of it is not 100% true, and they are blurring or conveniently omitting the truth. To some extent, this is unavoidable. In a system where being judged "able to work" depends on understanding the content after watching 30 minutes of TV, I sometimes have to be careful about what I say. Also, I have to recall and talk about what my worst days are like, which I am very bad at. I am used to downplaying how bad I feel, so this is very contrary to my instincts. However, as a follower of Jesus, I want to be honest, so I don't want to tell a blatant lie.

After thinking about this yesterday, I read that day's entry in Spargo's "Faith Checkbook," and the words seemed to leap off the page and speak to me.

"The reader must not, for a moment, try to escape difficulty through lies or questionable actions. Rather, continue to walk in the middle of the main road of truth and integrity. That is the best way. In life, do not take detours or consider tricks. Be just, and do not be afraid. Follow Jesus, and do not worry about bad outcomes. Even if the worst misfortune could be avoided through wicked deeds, the attempt itself would lead to a worse evil than any other misfortune. God's way must be the best way. Follow it, even if people think you are foolish. Then you will truly become wise."

So, I am praying, trying to trust, and doing my best to prepare without completely breaking down mentally. These benefit procedures are having a very negative impact on my mental health, and that is infuriating. Because I was finally starting to feel progress in Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), and I was happy because I felt I was becoming positive towards myself and finally recovering from depression.

I don't know if I want to win this appeal. If I win, I'll get some money, which isn't unwelcome, but at the same time, all the interviews and additional requirements for "getting back to work" will resume. Furthermore, there's talk of applying for more benefits, including Disability Living Allowance (DLA), and the thought of it is unbearable. It makes me feel like my life has no value. What am I even doing this for? On top of my poor health, this immense stress makes life seem so unappealing. This is the worst for mental health. DWP makes me feel like a worthless person, makes me feel guilty, makes me feel like a useless person, and makes me feel like a fraud at various levels (for example, by knitting and knitting a lot). I hate this system. It feels like being kicked when I'm already down.

I think I am truly spineless for making such a fuss over this.

text: 好きなだけ通報しろ。あれを破壊行為と呼ぶこと自体がそもそも馬鹿げてる。規約に反していようがいまいが、そんなものをわざわざ探してるお前は救いようのない馬鹿だ。おかげでWikipediaがずっと素晴らしい場所になったんだろうな。皮肉だぞ。さあ、BANしろ。
translation: Report as much as you want. The very act of calling that vandalism is ridiculous. Whether it violates the rules or not, you who are deliberately looking for such things are hopeless idiots. Thanks to you, Wikipedia has probably become a wonderful place. That's irony. Now, ban them.

text:
这个世界上有一个地方，只需一瞬间，就能让你所有的痛苦、糟糕的回忆和失望全部烟消云散。那个地方比你曾经去过的任何地方都好，而且就在你的掌心中。欢迎来到 Escorts Earls Court，这里是真正的人间天堂，是那些热爱美丽性感女性陪伴的真男人的乐园。Earls Court Agency 的创立，就是为了让绝美女性来照顾那些痛苦和失望的男人，将他们的生活改变得更好、更幸福。

Tell us about your ideal girl; if you have already chosen one, please tell us her name; if not, please describe her, tell us what you expect from her and what you want to experience with her, and we will know which girl is right for you.

美东时间4月9日周六晚上8点。嘿，大家好！😀 为了庆祝《Time Troopers》的发行，Jacob和我将在本周六美东时间晚上8点举办一场虚拟发布派对，届时我们将与共同编辑Christopher Ruocchio聊天，还会赠送签名本！如果你想了解更多信息并尝试赢取书籍，请加入我们 […]

就是今天！⌛ 如果你是经典或现代时间旅行故事的粉丝，Baen Books为你准备了一部选集——由Hank Davis和Christopher Ruocchio编辑的《Time Troopers》。我的作品《Doctor Quiet》为你抢先呈现了在即将出版的《The Janus File》中会遇到的新角色，充满了动作场面，它与来自……的故事并列。

这个夏天对有声书来说是个好夏天——尤其是对于我们的《Gordian Division》粉丝来说。😀 这件事甚至让我们感到意外！😮 但在漫长的等待之后，《The Valkyrie Protocol》（《Gordian Division》系列第二部）（同样由Baen Books出版，由优秀的Gabriel Vaughan朗读）的有声书版本终于发行并准备好 […]

嘿，读者们！如果你是某个事物的粉丝并且喜欢用有趣的方式发挥你的粉丝影响力，那么让我向你介绍一下 The Dragon Awards。由 Dragon Con 主办，The Dragon Awards 是年度奖项，旨在表彰当年在科幻和奇幻娱乐领域的最佳作品——包括书籍、电影、电视、漫画、 […]

欢呼吧，《Gordian Division》的粉丝们，因为你们的未来还有另一场直播！😀 美东时间10月7日周三下午4点，David和Jacob将在 Publishers Weekly 的作者活动系列 Books on Tap Live 中聊聊《The Valkyrie Protocol》！如果你想在互联网的魔力下，花一个惬意的夜晚与作者们一起闲逛， […]

这里有更多来自 The Weberverse 的内容！这一次，David和Jacob一起聊《The Valkyrie Protocol》，从整个冒险是如何开始的，到写作过程的细节。

嘿，《Gordian Division》的粉丝们！如果你在YouTube上并且想抢先预览《The Gordian Protocol》即将出版的续作，请前往 The Weberverse 观看 David 对《The Valkyrie Protocol》第一章的朗读！😄 ……或者你也可以直接在下面观看。😜 （别忘了，它将于2020年10月6日由 […]
translation:
Saturday, April 9th at 8 PM Eastern Time. Hey everyone! 😀 To celebrate the release of "Time Troopers," Jacob and I will be hosting a virtual launch party this Saturday at 8 PM Eastern Time, where we will chat with co-editor Christopher Ruocchio and give away signed copies! If you want to learn more and try to win a book, join us […]

It's today! ⌛ If you are a fan of classic or modern time travel stories, Baen Books has prepared a collection for you—"Time Troopers," edited by Hank Davis and Christopher Ruocchio. My work "Doctor Quiet" introduces a new character you will encounter in the upcoming "The Janus File," full of action, and stands alongside stories from……

This summer has been a good summer for audiobooks—especially for our "Gordian Division" fans. 😀 This even surprised us! 😮 But after a long wait, the audiobook version of "The Valkyrie Protocol" (Book two in the "Gordian Division" series) (also published by Baen Books and narrated by the excellent Gabriel Vaughan) is finally out and ready […]

Hey readers! If you are a fan of something and like to use your fandom influence in a fun way, then let me introduce you to The Dragon Awards. Hosted by Dragon Con, The Dragon Awards is an annual award that recognizes the best works in science fiction and fantasy entertainment of the year—including books, movies, TV, comics, […]

Cheer on, "Gordian Division" fans, because you have another live stream coming up! 😀 On Wednesday, October 7th at 4 PM Eastern Time, David and Jacob will chat about "The Valkyrie Protocol" on Publishers Weekly's author event series Books on Tap Live! If you want to spend a cozy evening hanging out with the authors under the magic of the internet, […]

Here is more content from The Weberverse! This time, David and Jacob chat about "The Valkyrie Protocol," from how the whole adventure started to the details of the writing process.

Hey, "Gordian Division" fans! If you are on YouTube and want a sneak peek of the upcoming sequel to "The Gordian Protocol," head over to The Weberverse to watch David read the first chapter of "The Valkyrie Protocol"! 😄 ……Or you can watch it directly below. 😜 (Don't forget, it will be released by […] on October 6, 2020)

外面得停止这么热了。
你看上去像是在做些什么，但你和其他人一样烂。
translation:

It has to stop being this hot outside.
You look like you're doing something, but you're as bad as everyone else.

text:
马萨诸塞州布里奇沃特——周六下午，在Rosen纪念网球场上，罗德岛学院男子网球队以9-0的大比分完胜布里奇沃特州立大学，在小东部联盟（Little East Conference）的比赛中保持不败。

罗德岛学院 9, 布里奇沃特州立大学 0
（罗德岛学院 6-1, 2-0 LEC）
（布里奇沃特州立大学 0-2, 0-2 LEC）

比赛进程
• RIC赢下了全部三场双打比赛，早早取得了3-0的领先优势。
• 大一新生Cam Chadwick（罗德岛州纳拉甘塞特）和Mike Dickie（罗德岛州史密斯菲尔德）以8-0击败布里奇沃特州立大学的大三学生Michael Scollins（马萨诸塞州马什菲尔德）和大四学生Dan O'Malley（马萨诸塞州达克斯伯里），为RIC拿下了当天的第一个积分。
• 当大四学生Brett Taggart（罗德岛州北金斯敦）和大一新生Alex Bourque（罗德岛州北史密斯菲尔德）在双打第一场以8-2击败布里奇沃特州立大学的大四学生Justin Haley（马萨诸塞州南亚茅斯）和大二学生Joshua Bean（马萨诸塞州金斯顿）时，Anchormen队将比分扩大为2-0。
• 大三学生Matthew Gambardella（罗德岛州西沃里克）和大一新生Ethan Hoy（罗德岛州西沃里克）在双打第三场以8-3击败Bears队的大三学生Jonathan Pereira（马萨诸塞州福尔河）和大四学生Zachary Mutti（马萨诸塞州皮博迪），完成了双打比赛的横扫。
• Gambardella在单打第四场以一场令人印象深刻的(6-0, 6-0)完胜Pereira，使RIC取得了4-0的领先。
• Dickie在单打第六场以(6-0, 6-1)击败Bean，为Anchormen队锁定了下午的第五个积分，也是决定性的积分。
• Taggart在单打第二场以(6-2, 6-1)击败Scollins，Bourque在对阵O'Malley的比赛中以(6-1, 6-4)获胜，使RIC的比分达到7-0。
• Anchormen队以Hoy在单打第五场(6-4, 6-0)击败Mutti结束了当天的比赛，而Chadwick在单打第一场以(6-1, 6-1)击败Haley，完成了对整场比赛的横扫。
• 在接下来的比赛中，罗德岛学院将于4月9日（周一）下午3:30主场迎战Lesley。
translation:
Bridgewater, Massachusetts – On Saturday afternoon, the Rhode Island College men's tennis team decisively defeated Bridgewater State University 9-0 at the Rosen Tennis Courts, maintaining their undefeated streak in the Little East Conference.

Rhode Island College 9, Bridgewater State University 0
(RIC 6-1, 2-0 LEC)
(BSU 0-2, 0-2 LEC)

Match Progression
• RIC won all three doubles matches, taking an early 3-0 lead.
• Freshman Cam Chadwick (Narragansett, Rhode Island) and Mike Dickie (Smithfield, Rhode Island) defeated senior Michael Scollins (Mashfield, Massachusetts) and senior Dan O'Malley (Duxbury, Massachusetts) 8-0, securing RIC's first point of the day.
• When senior Brett Taggart (North Kingstown, Rhode Island) and freshman Alex Bourque (North Smithfield, Rhode Island) defeated senior Justin Haley (South Yarmouth, Massachusetts) and sophomore Joshua Bean (Kensington, Massachusetts) 8-2 in the first doubles match, the Anchormen extended their lead to 2-0.
• Senior Matthew Gambardella (West Warwick, Rhode Island) and freshman Ethan Hoy (West Warwick, Rhode Island) swept the doubles matches by defeating senior Jonathan Pereira (Forrest, Massachusetts) and senior Zachary Mutti (Pawtucket, Massachusetts) 8-3 in the third doubles match.
• Gambardella defeated Pereira in the fourth singles match with an impressive (6-0, 6-0) victory, giving RIC a 4-0 lead.
• Dickie defeated Bean in the sixth singles match (6-0, 6-1), securing the Anchormen's fifth and decisive point of the afternoon.
• Taggart defeated Scollins in the second singles match (6-2, 6-1), and Bourque defeated O'Malley in his match (6-1, 6-4), bringing RIC's score to 7-0.
• The Anchormen concluded the day's matches with Hoy defeating Mutti in the fifth singles match (6-4, 6-0), while Chadwick completed the sweep of the entire match by defeating Haley in the first singles match (6-1, 6-1).
• In the next match, Rhode Island College will host Lesley on Monday, April 9th, at 3:30 PM.

text:
在我生命中的不同阶段，观察力敏锐的陌生人仅通过看我手写的东西就能猜出我的职业。我能告诉你的只有两种可以实现这种技巧的职业。我先从简单的那个开始：如果字迹看起来像乱涂乱画且无法辨认，人们可能会猜作者是一名医生，因为太忙了而顾不上字形。然后就是建筑师。那么，揭示作者是建筑师的特征是什么？是什么让建筑师的字迹与他人不同？建筑学和手写字之间真的有联系吗？

出于某种我无法确定的原因，建筑师是在“绘字”而非“写字”。我怀疑这种语义上的差异仅仅是为了更强调实际字母的笔触和形状。人们经常观察到，建筑师倾向于使用“印刷体”而非“草书”。这似乎起源于所有建筑图纸和文字都由手工完成的时代，而印刷体方块字显然比草写体更容易阅读。由于建筑师的图纸被用于建造房屋，因此清晰易读至关重要。建筑师还倾向于全部使用大写字母（这并不总是被视为在尖叫！），同样是为了可读性。然而，最能区分建筑绘字特征的是其字形是风格化的。这里有一个小秘密（或者也许根本不是秘密）：建筑绘字并非“自然”产生。它是经过精心打造的。在 Doug Patt 的经典视频《如何像建筑师一样写字》中可以找到关于这个过程的极佳解释。

建筑师风格化的绘字与我们的同事——工程师的字迹形成对比。工程师在图纸和文字中追求一致性和统一性，而建筑师则在较宽松的限制下追求创造力和个性。通过考虑笔画的倾斜度、主干的垂直度、相似字符的形状、比例以及字符之间的间距，人们可以打造出一种个人绘字“风格”。在建筑师完全手工绘图和绘字的年代，人们仅凭绘字方式就能识别出哪些建筑师参与了哪些图纸。建筑师发展出了自己的绘图风格。Richard Neutra 开发了一种设计精良且极具辨识度的绘字风格，以至于被当代字体铸造厂 House Industries 所采用。这一类别的其他建筑师还包括 Frank Lloyd Wright 和 Charles Rennie Macintosh。讽刺的是，这种向字体的转换使得这些建筑师创造的文字变得既一致又统一。

建筑师在绘字中投入的设计考量也延伸到了其他领域。建筑师展现其手写灵巧度的另一种方式体现在他们的签名中。很多建筑师拥有非常酷的签名。无论字符是否可辨，这些签名通常都是精心打造的设计。Renzo Piano 和 Ralph Raison 是我最喜欢的两位。

因此，图纸、绘字和签名与建筑师的个人风格紧密相连。这种风格的最终体现形式就是一座建筑，而没有任何一种建筑类型比博物馆更令建筑师向往。这就是我们将要探讨的下一个建筑关联。请与我一起关注《艺术或建筑》。
translation:
In different stages of my life, strangers with keen observation skills could guess my profession just by looking at my handwriting. I can tell you there are only two professions that can achieve this skill. I'll start with the simpler one: if the handwriting looks like scribbles and is illegible, people might guess the author is a doctor who is too busy to worry about letter formation. Then there are architects. So, what are the characteristics that reveal the author is an architect? What makes an architect's handwriting different from others? Is there really a connection between architecture and handwriting?

For some reason I cannot determine, architects are "drawing" rather than "writing." I suspect this semantic difference is merely to emphasize the strokes and shapes of the actual letters. People often observe that architects tend to use "print" rather than "cursive." This seems to originate from the era when all architectural drawings and text were done by hand, and block print was clearly easier to read than cursive. Since architects' drawings are used to build houses, clarity and readability are crucial. Architects also tend to use all capital letters (which is not always seen as shouting!), also for readability. However, what most distinguishes the architectural drawing style is that its letterforms are stylized. Here is a little secret (or maybe not a secret at all): architectural drawing is not "naturally" produced. It is meticulously crafted. An excellent explanation of this process can be found in Doug Patt's classic video, "How to Write Like an Architect."

The stylized drawing of architects contrasts with the handwriting of our colleagues—engineers. Engineers pursue consistency and uniformity in drawings and text, while architects pursue creativity and individuality under looser constraints. By considering the slant of the strokes, the verticality of the main lines, the shape of similar characters, proportions, and the spacing between characters, one can develop a personal drawing "style." In the era when architects drew and wrote entirely by hand, people could identify which architects were involved in which drawings just by their drawing style. Architects developed their own drawing styles. Richard Neutra developed a well-designed and highly recognizable drawing style that was adopted by the contemporary type foundry House Industries. Other architects in this category include Frank Lloyd Wright and Charles Rennie Mackintosh. Ironically, this transition to typography made the text created by these architects both consistent and unified.

The design considerations an architect puts into drawing also extend to other areas. Another way architects demonstrate their manual dexterity is in their signatures. Many architects have very cool signatures. Regardless of whether the characters are legible, these signatures are usually carefully designed. Renzo Piano and Ralph Raison are my favorites.

Therefore, drawings, drawing, and signatures are closely linked to an architect's personal style. The ultimate manifestation of this style is a building, and no building type is more desired by architects than a museum. This is the next architectural connection we will explore. Please follow along with me on "Art or Architecture."

text:
美国支持的库尔德和阿拉伯战士在叙利亚北部开辟了对抗伊斯兰国（IS）组织的新战线。
报道称，叙利亚民主力量（SDF）正向土耳其边境附近的IS控制城镇曼比季推进，旨在切断该极端组织用于跨境运送物资和外国战士的区域。
总部位于英国的叙利亚人权观察组织6月1日表示，SDF部队已控制16个村庄，距离曼比季约15公里。
该组织还表示，由美国领导的国际联盟在黎明前对曼比季发动的空袭造成15名平民死亡。
SDF顾问纳赛尔·哈吉·曼苏尔（Nasser Haj Mansour）表示，冲突“激烈且猛烈”。
据引述匿名美国官员的话称，此次行动部署了数千名部队，可能会持续数周。
他们表示，少数美国特种作战部队将作为顾问在地面支持此次推进。
基于法新社（AFP）、路透社（Reuters）和美联社（AP）的报道。
translation:
Kurdish and Arab fighters supported by the United States have opened a new front against the Islamic State (IS) organization in northern Syria.
Reports say the Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) are advancing toward Manbij, an IS-controlled town near the Turkish border, aiming to cut off the area used by the extremist group for cross-border transport of supplies and foreign fighters.
The UK-based Syrian Observatory for Human Rights stated on June 1 that SDF forces have controlled 16 villages, about 15 kilometers from Manbij.
The organization also reported that airstrikes launched before dawn on Manbij by the US-led international coalition killed 15 civilians.
SDF advisor Nasser Haj Mansour said the conflict was "fierce and intense."
According to anonymous US officials quoted, the operation has deployed thousands of troops and may last for several weeks.
They said a small number of US special operations forces will provide ground support as advisors for this advance.
Based on reports from AFP, Reuters, and AP.

经上写道：“主说：‘我活着指证，万膝必向我跪拜；万口必向神承认。’” 罗马书 14:11, TNIV
我们阅读圣经并知道，当耶稣再次降临地时，每个人都将受到审判。耶稣将证明自己是唯一真神，所有嘲讽者都将心甘情愿地在他面前跪拜，承认他是主。
现在嘲讽耶稣的人没有意识到他是多么强大，有时他们的态度会影响我们，尽管我们并不希望如此。我们忘记了《启示录》中口出宝剑的耶稣，而转而关注那个想要祝福小孩子的“温柔”耶稣。面对那些宣扬进化论、冷静且逻辑严密的无神论者，以及那些声称通往上帝有多种途径的魅力演说家，我们低估了耶稣的力量和威能。

text:
2009年12月25日
当我们回顾过去十年（就此而言，也包括过去一年）新闻价值的衰减时，我们可能会看到重新定义什么是新闻的机会。这个想法源于美联社（AP）今天发布的关于华盛顿州年度头条新闻的报告。
换句话说，这些是全州范围内今年最大的、最具新闻价值的故事。（每个州，或者至少几乎所有州，每年都会汇总类似的清单。）
第一名是11月四名莱克伍德警察被枪击的事件。毫无疑问，这是一个强有力的、戏剧性的、令人心碎的故事；它确实是重大新闻。这件事有些不寻常，因为这次事件是对当时甚至没有在与公众互动的警察的伏击。
但世界，或者华盛顿州，或者其中的大部分，因此而改变了吗？我们学到了什么非常新东西吗？在这种情况下，一个疯子决定杀掉警察；我们大多数人可能都知道，（遗憾地，确实）这就是这个世界的现状。
第二个故事是选民批准了“除婚姻以外的一切法规”。那是该州的一次重大变革，标志着全国文化观念的显著转变。第三个故事是该州9%以上的失业率——这是一个巨大的新现实，正面对并影响着（直接或间接）所有华盛顿州居民。第四个故事是《西雅图邮报-情报报》（Seattle Post-Intelligencer）停止纸质运营——这是该州通信、新闻报道、政治和经济的一个重大转折点。第五个故事是波音公司决定将新的787生产线迁至南卡罗来纳州，这一进展对普吉特海湾具有大规模的影响。
那么，什么是新闻？优先级是什么？也许，在这个动荡的时代，我们可能想要停下来思考一下。分享到Facebook
translation:
December 25, 2009
As we look back over the past decade (and by extension, the past year) at the decline in news value, we might see an opportunity to redefine what news is. This idea stems from a report released today by the Associated Press (AP) on the Washington State annual top stories.
In other words, these are the biggest, most newsworthy stories across the entire state this year. (Every state, or at least almost every state, compiles a similar list annually.)
Number one was the shooting of four Lakewood police officers in November. Undeniably, this was a powerful, dramatic, heartbreaking story; it was major news. This incident was somewhat unusual because it was an ambush on police officers who were not even interacting with the public at the time.
But did the world, or Washington State, or most of it, change because of it? Did we learn anything truly new? In this case, a madman decided to kill police officers; most of us probably already knew, (regrettably, it is) the state of the world.
The second story was voters approving "everything except marriage." That was a major shift in the state, marking a significant change in national cultural attitudes. The third story was the state's unemployment rate of over 9%—a huge new reality confronting and affecting (directly or indirectly) all Washington residents. The fourth story was the Seattle Post-Intelligencer ceasing its print operations—a major turning point for communication, journalism, politics, and the economy in the state. The fifth story was Boeing's decision to move its new 787 production line to South Carolina, a development with large-scale implications for the Puget Sound.
So, what is news? What is the priority? Perhaps, in this turbulent age, we might want to pause and think about it. Share to Facebook

text:
|快采取点行动吧，好吗？！|
海豚队这支球队有5个显而易见的漏洞。不分先后分别是：后卫（Guard）、跑卫（Running Back）、角卫（Cornerback）、线卫（Linebacker）和防守端锋（Defensive End）。你可以因为签下了 Andre Branch 而争论说防守端锋不再是需求，但在这个联盟里，你需要尽可能多地拥有冲传手。我知道我是少数派，但我并不信任 Cam Wake。我认为 Wake 这个年纪（34岁）加上这次受伤，已经完蛋了，过气了，处于职业生涯的下滑期。他再也回不到以前的状态了，如果一场比赛你能从他那里得到15-20次出场机会，就得认为海豚队走运了。我甚至觉得他明年给不了我们这么多。
现在，你不可能在一个休赛期解决所有这些需求。海豚队在重建，我明白。他们不想用“重建”这个词，但他们就是在这么做。而且他们应该这么做，再次强调，这没问题。但是，海豚队需要一些“创可贴”来修补其中一些漏洞，以便撑过2016赛季并成为一支体面的球队。那么，在我上面列出的5个漏洞中，最容易解决的2个是什么？很简单，跑卫和后卫。如果我管理海豚队，我希望在进入这次选秀时，完全不需要担心必须选跑卫或后卫，除非有极好的机会直接掉到他们怀里。这两个位置海豚队可以通过自由市场相对便宜地填补，签一个老将的一年合同，只要能撑过接下来的这个赛季，然后把“必须需求”推到下一个休赛期。
接下来是后卫位置。听着，作为海豚队的球迷，我们以前见过这种把戏，管理层完全忽视这个位置，对 Billy Turner, Dallas Thomas 和 Jamil Douglas 这种烂球员寄予过多的信心。那三个人在这个联盟里根本没法打。我不需要再看他们一眼就知道这一点。如果海豚队指望他们中的任何一个人能做出什么贡献，那么这个球队就没救了。我能理解海豚队不想为了像 Alex Boone 这样的人花大钱，我明白。他拿到了一份没人预料到的合同，海豚队不想为一个后卫付那么多钱。但海豚队必须采取行动，签一个经历过重大伤病、从未打过后卫的破烂老截锋，并指望他解决这个问题，简直蠢到家了。我是说真的非常非常蠢。简直是 2 + 2 = 9 那种蠢！Jermon Bushrod 现在充其量是个年老的替补截锋，如果他还能健康的话。再次强调，海豚队无法修复阵容上的每一个漏洞。去签一个一年合同的老将后卫（外面还有很多有能力的球员），在进入选秀前完全把选后卫的需求从议程中剔除。
迈阿密在即将到来的选秀中无法修复5个位置，但他们绝对可以修复其中的3个！在跑卫位置找个“创可贴”，在后卫位置找个“创可贴”。进入选秀，在角卫、线卫和冲传手身上加倍下注。你希望在这个休赛期纠正这些位置，然后明年，你不会被任何跑卫或高薪后卫绑死，然后重新评估这些位置的情况并继续前进。这太简单了，但迈阿密正任由优质球员的市场枯竭，他们在三月份就在破坏自己的赛季，看着真痛苦。没有一个理性的海豚队球迷指望球队今年能冲击季后赛，但我也不想看到一支只赢2场或3场比赛的球队，而这正是他们目前走上的道路。这不是过度消极，这是现实。
我知道现在肯定有一两个海豚队球迷在说……“要有耐心，现在才三月。”听着，在 NFL，增加人才的窗口非常小。你在三月份大约有2周时间，然后在四月份的选秀中有3天。在自由市场中，真正的“人才”在大约一周内就会枯竭，之后剩下的就是试图挽救 NFL 职业生涯的破烂老球员，或者充其量只是些填补空缺的角色球员，不能指望他们能给球队带来影响。现在跑卫市场正在迅速枯竭，而后卫市场还没有。
translation:
Take some action already, okay?!
The Dolphins have five obvious holes. In no particular order, they are: Guard, Running Back, Cornerback, Linebacker, and Defensive End. You can argue that the defensive end is no longer a need because they signed Andre Branch, but in this league, you need as many rushers as possible. I know I'm in the minority, but I don't trust Cam Wake. I think Wake at his age (34) plus this injury, is done, past his prime, in a career decline. He can't get back to his former level; if you get 15-20 snaps from him in a game, you should consider the Dolphins lucky. I even think he won't give us that much next year.
Now, you can't solve all these needs in an offseason. The Dolphins are rebuilding, I get it. They don't want to use the word "rebuild," but that's what they are doing. And they should be, to reiterate, that's fine. But the Dolphins need some "band-aids" to patch up some of these holes to get through the 2016 season and be a respectable team. So, out of the five holes I listed, what are the two easiest to fix? Simple, running back and guard. If I were managing the Dolphins, I would want to enter the draft without having to worry about needing to draft a running back or a guard, unless there's an extremely good opportunity that falls right into their lap. The Dolphins can fill these two positions relatively cheaply on the free market, signing a veteran for a one-year deal, just to get through the next season and push the "must-need" to the next offseason.
Next is the guard position. Listen, as a Dolphins fan, we've seen this trick before—management completely ignoring this position and putting too much faith in bad players like Billy Turner, Dallas Thomas, and Jamil Douglas. Those three guys are useless in this league. I don't even need to look at them to know that. If the Dolphins are counting on any of them to make a contribution, then this team is beyond saving. I understand the Dolphins don't want to spend big money on someone like Alex Boone, I get it. He got an unexpected contract, and the Dolphins don't want to pay that much for a guard. But the Dolphins must take action, signing a worn-out veteran tackle who has had major injuries and never played guard, and expecting him to solve this problem—that's just stupid. I mean really, really stupid. Like 2 + 2 = 9 stupid! Jermon Bushrod is at best an aging backup tackle, if he's even healthy. To reiterate, the Dolphins cannot fix every single hole in the roster. Sign a veteran guard on a one-year deal (there are plenty of capable players out there) and completely remove the need for a guard from the agenda before the draft.
Miami can't fix 5 positions in the upcoming draft, but they absolutely can fix 3 of them! Find a "band-aid" at running back, find a "band-aid" at guard. Enter the draft and double down on cornerbacks, linebackers, and rushers. You want to correct these positions in this offseason, so next year, you aren't tied down by any running back or high-priced guard, and then re-evaluate the situation at these positions and move forward. It's that simple, but Miami is letting quality players dry up on the market; they are sabotaging their season in March, and it's painful to watch. No rational Dolphins fan expects the team to make the playoffs this year, but I don't want to see a team that only wins 2 or 3 games, which is exactly where they are headed. This isn't being overly negative; this is reality.
I know there are definitely one or two Dolphins fans right now saying... "Be patient, it's only March." Listen, in the NFL, the window to add talent is very small. You have about two weeks in March, and then three days in the April draft. In the free market, real "talent" dries up in about a week; after that, it's just broken old players trying to salvage an NFL career, or at best, role players to fill gaps, and you can't expect them to make a difference for the team. The running back market is rapidly drying up now, and the guard market hasn't yet.

text:
法国法院维持了一项禁止一名穆斯林工程师进入核电站的禁令，理由是该穆斯林与所谓的“圣战网络”有联系，但其律师称这是一个伊斯兰恐惧症的案例。
律师 Sefen Guez Guez 周一告诉法新社，他正考虑提起上诉。
这名 29 岁的穆斯林在一家由能源巨头 EDF 分包的公司工作，在 2012 年和 2013 年期间，作为工作的一部分，他被允许进入核设施。
但今年 3 月，根据法国法律不能透露姓名的该男子进入 Nogent-sur-Seine 核电站的通行证被吊销。
官员们表示，他与一个暴力武装组织有联系，并且与一名参与招募人员前往伊拉克作战的伊玛目有接触。
位于东北部城镇 Chalons-en-Champagne 的一家法院维持了该禁令，称管理层可以防止那些“处于政治和宗教激进化过程中的人”进入敏感场所。
该男子的律师对此表示愤慨，并辩称其当事人没有犯罪记录。
2014 年 6 月，Guez Guez 成功地通过上诉法院撤销了禁令。但当这名工程师去上班时，他发现自己再次被拒绝进入工作场所——这次是由 EDF 拒绝的，于是他的律师再次提起上诉。
法国拥有约 500 万穆斯林——这是西欧最大的穆斯林群体，其中有许多暴力的“禁区”，那里经常发生焚车事件。
与许多欧洲国家一样，法国对穆斯林离开该国前往伊拉克和叙利亚作战表示担忧，并认为他们返回后将对国内安全构成重大风险。
根据官方估计，约 800 名法国国民或居民（包括数十名女性）前往叙利亚从事恐怖活动。
translation:
A French court upheld a ban preventing a Muslim engineer from entering a nuclear power plant, citing the Muslim's alleged ties to a so-called "jihadist network," but his lawyer called it a case of Islamophobia.
Lawyer Sefen Guez Guez told AFP on Monday that he is considering an appeal.
The 29-year-old Muslim works for a company subcontracted by the energy giant EDF, and was allowed access to nuclear facilities as part of his job in 2012 and 2013.
However, in March of this year, the pass of the man, whose name cannot be disclosed under French law, to enter the Nogent-sur-Seine nuclear power plant was revoked.
Officials stated that he had ties to a violent armed group and had been in contact with an imam who was involved in recruiting people to fight in Iraq.
A court in the northeastern town of Chalons-en-Champagne upheld the ban, stating that management can prevent people "in the process of political and religious radicalization" from entering sensitive sites.
The man's lawyer expressed outrage over this, arguing that his client has no criminal record.
In June 2014, Guez Guez successfully overturned the ban through the court of appeal. But when the engineer went to work, he found himself denied entry to the workplace again—this time by EDF, so his lawyer appealed again.
France has about 5 million Muslims—the largest Muslim community in Western Europe—and many "no-go zones" where car burnings frequently occur.
Like many European countries, France is concerned about Muslims leaving the country to fight in Iraq and Syria, believing they pose a significant risk to domestic security upon their return.
According to official estimates, about 800 French nationals or residents (including dozens of women) have traveled to Syria to engage in terrorist activities.

您可能会考虑光顾这家店。但是，它值得您花钱吗？低价可能是一个陷阱，旨在让您放松警惕。就像俗话说的“太好而不真实”，这家店可能也是如此。

text:
Common Cause, Christy Setzer, 617-512-7572；
Lawyers' Committee, Stacie Royster, 202-662-8317
华盛顿特区 —— 民权和投票权组织今日赞扬 Clear Channel Corporation 同意拆除在克利夫兰、哥伦布和密尔沃基以非裔美国人和拉丁裔为主的社区中设置的一系列广告牌。在过去的一周里，这些组织发起了一场活动，说服 Clear Channel 拆除这些广告牌。这些广告牌警告投票欺诈将受到刑事处罚，旨在给少数族裔选民贴上污名并恐吓他们。这些广告牌是由匿名资助的。
“对于我们这些关心保护投票权的人来说，这是一个不可否认的胜利，”俄亥俄州 Common Cause 的 Catherine Turcer 表示。“允许人们恐吓选民并干扰投票是不符合美国精神的。现在，Norton Outdoor 应该效仿 Clear Channel，也将那些匿名资助的广告牌拆除。”Norton Outdoor 是在辛辛那提地区设置广告牌的公司。Clear Channel 的广告牌则位于密尔沃基、哥伦布和克利夫兰市场。
根据 Clear Channel 向《克利夫兰平民报》（Cleveland Plain-Dealer）发表的声明，该公司最终同意拆除广告牌，是因为它允许这些广告牌在没有资助者免责声明的情况下挂出，违反了其披露政策。目前尚不清楚在辛辛那提设置广告牌的广告公司 Norton Outdoor 是否也会拆除其广告牌。
“我们一同赞赏 Clear Channel 做出拆除这些不雅广告牌的正确决定，”NEA 主席 Dennis Van Roekel 表示。“他们树立了正确的榜样。我们呼吁 Norton Outdoor 效仿 Clear Channel。我们需要大声且明确地表示——在我们的选举过程中，卑劣的手段和恐吓行为将不会被容忍。”
“对于反抗这些广告牌的社区来说，这是一个令人振奋的胜利，”Lawyers Committee for Civil Rights Under Law 的执行主任 Barbara Arnwine 表示。“言论自由是所有美国人的权利，公民们利用自己的声音来组织并反对这种有针对性的压制策略。Clear Channel 对抗议做出回应，做了正确的事。”
“Clear Channel 决定拆除恐吓性广告牌，是自由、公正且便捷选举的胜利，”Demos 的法律战略副总裁 Brenda Wright 表示。“Norton Outdoor 应该效仿，停止推广那些阻碍合资格美国人行使投票权的恐惧战术。”
Common Cause、Demos 和 Lawyers' Committee for Civil Rights Under Law 都是选举保护联盟（Election Protection coalition）的一部分。该联盟上周开始发起一项活动，用他们自己的支持投票信息来对抗那些匿名资助的广告牌。其中一块广告牌敦促居民“站出来，发出你的声音——投票”。第二组广告牌提醒选民“当我们投票时，我们所有人都是平等的”。这些广告牌还包含了联盟的选民热线电话 1-866-OUR-VOTE，该热线提供选民信息，并回答关于选民登记、身份证件要求和选举程序的疑问。该联盟还将在选举日于全国各地的投票站开展实地计划。
选举保护联盟通过全国选举保护热线 866-OUR-VOTE 为选民提供支持，这是一个由经过培训的志愿者 staffing 的选民信息热线。今年，选举保护联盟还推出了选举保护智能手机应用程序，任何选民都可以使用该程序检查其登记状态、查找投票地点或获取选民教育材料。选民可以通过向 90975 发送短信“OurVote”来下载选举保护智能手机应用程序。选民还可以访问 www.866OurVote.org，其中包含特定州的选举和投票信息。
办公室：Common Cause National
议题：投票与选举
Common Cause 是一个非党派的基层组织，致力于维护美国民主的核心价值
translation:
Common Cause, Christy Setzer, 617-512-7572;
Lawyers' Committee, Stacie Royster, 202-662-8317
WASHINGTON D.C. — Civil rights and voting rights organizations today praised Clear Channel Corporation for agreeing to remove a series of billboards placed in predominantly African American and Latino communities in Cleveland, Columbus, and Milwaukee. Over the past week, these organizations launched a campaign to persuade Clear Channel to take down these billboards. These billboards warned that voting fraud would be criminally prosecuted, aiming to stigmatize and intimidate minority voters. These billboards were anonymously funded.
“For those of us who care about protecting voting rights, this is an undeniable victory,” said Catherine Turcer of Common Cause in Ohio. “Allowing people to intimidate voters and interfere with voting is contrary to the American spirit. Now, Norton Outdoor should follow Clear Channel's lead and remove those anonymously funded billboards.” Norton Outdoor is a company that places billboards in the Cincinnati area. Clear Channel's billboards were located in the Milwaukee, Columbus, and Cleveland markets.
According to a statement released by Clear Channel to the Cleveland Plain-Dealer, the company ultimately agreed to remove the billboards because they were posted without a funder disclaimer, violating its disclosure policy. It is currently unclear whether Norton Outdoor, the billboard company in Cincinnati, will also remove its billboards.
“We commend Clear Channel for making the right decision to remove these offensive billboards,” said Dennis Van Roekel, Chairman of the NEA. “They have set the right example. We call on Norton Outdoor to follow Clear Channel's lead. We need to state loudly and clearly—that in our elections, vile tactics and intimidation will not be tolerated.”
“For the communities resisting these billboards, this is an inspiring victory,” said Barbara Arnwine, Executive Director of the Lawyers Committee for Civil Rights Under Law. “Freedom of speech is a right of all Americans, and citizens are using their voices to organize and oppose this targeted suppression strategy. Clear Channel did the right thing by responding to the protest.”
“Clear Channel's decision to remove intimidating billboards is a victory for free, fair, and accessible elections,” said Brenda Wright, Vice President of Legal Strategy at Demos. “Norton Outdoor should follow suit and stop promoting fear tactics that impede eligible Americans from exercising their right to vote.”
Common Cause, Demos, and Lawyers' Committee for Civil Rights Under Law are all part of the Election Protection coalition. The coalition began a campaign last week using its own supportive voting information to counter the anonymously funded billboards. One billboard urged residents to “Speak up, make your voice heard—vote.” Another set of billboards reminded voters that “When we vote, we are all equal.” These billboards also included the coalition's voter hotline 1-866-OUR-VOTE, which provides voter information and answers questions about voter registration, ID requirements, and election procedures. The coalition will also have in-person plans at polling places nationwide on Election Day.
The Election Protection coalition provides support to voters through the national Election Protection hotline 866-OUR-VOTE, a voter information hotline staffed by trained volunteers. This year, the Election Protection coalition also launched the Election Protection mobile app, which any voter can use to check their registration status, find polling places, or access voter education materials. Voters can download the Election Protection mobile app by texting “OurVote” to 90975. Voters can also visit www.866OurVote.org, which contains election and voting information for specific states.
Office: Common Cause National
Issue: Voting and Elections
Common Cause is a non-partisan grassroots organization dedicated to upholding the core values of American democracy.

text:
布鲁塞尔 (JTA) —— 在法国法院强制 Twitter 删除部分反犹太内容一年多后，专家表示，该裁决产生了连锁反应，导致其他互联网公司为了避免诉讼，在打击仇恨言论方面采取了更激进的行动。

巴黎上诉法院 2013 年的裁决解决了一起由法国犹太学生联盟在前一年发起的诉讼。该诉讼针对的是标签 #UnBonJuif（意为“一个好犹太人”），该标签被用于索引数千条违反法国反仇恨言论法的反犹太评论。

此后，YouTube 永久封禁了 Dieudonne 发布的视频，这位法国喜剧演员曾 10 次因煽动针对犹太人的种族仇恨而被定罪。据该公司称，今年 2 月，Facebook 删除了法国大屠杀否认者 Alain Soral 的页面，原因是其“重复发布不符合 Facebook 条款的内容”。Soral 的页面在过去几年中引发了许多投诉，但直到今年才被删除。

“大公司不想被起诉，”前学者、现任互联网协会（Internet Society，一个鼓励各国政府确保互联网访问和可持续使用的国际组织）政策顾问的 Konstantinos Komaitis 表示。“因此，在法国的裁决之后，我们看到像 Google、YouTube、Facebook 这样的互联网服务提供商倾向于尝试调整他们的服务条款——即他们自己的内部法理——以确保他们遵守国家法律。”

这一变化发生在欧洲法院对利用在线平台煽动种族主义或暴力的个人下达一系列重刑之际。

周一，一家英国法院判处一名此类违规者四周监禁，因为他向一名犹太议员推特发送了“希特勒是对的”。上周，日内瓦的一家法院判处一名男子五个月监禁，因为他发布了否认大屠杀的文字。今年 4 月，一家法国法院判处两名男子五个月监禁，因为他们发布了一段反犹太视频。

“更严厉的判决部分源于法官意识到在线仇恨所带来的危险，同时也考虑到网络圣战主义以及它如何影响像 Mohammed Merah 这样的人，”比利时反反犹太联盟的法律顾问 Christophe Goossens 说道，他指的是 2012 年在图卢兹一所犹太学校杀害四名犹太人的凶手。

在 Twitter 案中，该公司辩称作为一家美国公司，它受到第一修正案的保护。但法院驳回了这一论点，并强制 Twitter 删除部分评论并识别部分作者。法院还要求该公司建立一套系统，用于标记并最终删除违反仇恨言论法的评论。

Twitter 全球安全外展和公共政策负责人 Patricias Cartes Andres 周一在由国际反网络仇恨网络 (INACH) 在布鲁塞尔组织的一次会议上透露，Twitter 通过全面修订其服务条款，以促进对欧洲法律的遵守。

“规则已经过修改，使我们能够在特定群体被针对时删除更多内容，”Cartes Andres 告诉 JTA。她补充说，在诉讼之前，“如果你没有针对任何一个人，你可能是可以逃脱处罚的。”

这一变化在五个月前生效，但 Twitter “希望对此保持低调，因为会有其他群体，比如言论自由群体，对此感到非常不满，因为他们会将其视为审查，”Cartes Andres 说道。

INACH 秘书 Suzette Bronkhorst 表示，Twitter 调整后的政策是 2013 年以来在线服务提供商“态度转变”的一部分。

“在审判之前，Twitter 对欧洲竖了中指，”Brokhorst 说道。“但他们意识到，如果他们想在欧洲开展业务，就需要遵守欧洲法律，其他人也意识到了这一点。”

根据 Komaitis 的说法，Twitter 案是基于 2000 年的一项里程碑式法院裁决，该裁决强制搜索引擎 Yahoo! 禁止销售纳粹
translation:
Brussels (JTA) — More than a year after a French court forced Twitter to remove some antisemitic content, experts say the ruling triggered a chain reaction, leading other internet companies to take more aggressive action against hate speech to avoid lawsuits.

The Paris Court of Appeal's 2013 ruling resolved a lawsuit filed by the French Jewish Students' Union the previous year. The lawsuit targeted the hashtag #UnBonJuif (meaning "a good Jew"), which was used to index thousands of antisemitic comments violating French anti-hate speech laws.

Subsequently, YouTube permanently banned videos posted by Dieudonne, a French comedian who had been convicted 10 times for inciting racial hatred against Jews. The company stated that in February of this year, Facebook deleted the page of French Holocaust denier Alain Soral because it was "repeatedly posting content that violates Facebook's terms." Soral's page had drawn many complaints over the past few years but was only deleted this year.

"Big companies don't want to be sued," said Konstantinos Komaitis, a former academic and current policy advisor at the Internet Society, an international organization that encourages governments worldwide to ensure internet access and sustainability. "Therefore, after the ruling in France, we saw internet service providers like Google, YouTube, and Facebook tending to try and adjust their terms of service—that is, their own internal jurisprudence—to ensure they comply with national laws."

This shift comes as the European Court has handed down a series of heavy sentences to individuals who incite racism or violence using online platforms.

On Monday, a UK court sentenced one such offender to four weeks in prison for tweeting "Hitler was right" to a Jewish MP. Last week, a court in Geneva sentenced a man to five months in prison for posting text denying the Holocaust. In April of this year, a French court sentenced two men to five months in prison for posting an antisemitic video.

"The harsher sentences are partly due to judges realizing the danger of online hate, while also considering cyber-jihadism and how it affects people like Mohammed Merah," said Christophe Goossens, legal counsel for the Belgian Anti-Defamation League, referring to the perpetrator who killed four Jews in a Toulouse Jewish school in 2012.

In the Twitter case, the company argued that as an American company, it was protected by the First Amendment. However, the court rejected this argument and ordered Twitter to remove some comments and identify some authors. The court also required the company to establish a system to flag and ultimately delete comments that violate hate speech laws.

Patricias Cartes Andres, Head of Global Safety Outreach and Public Policy at Twitter, revealed at a meeting organized by the International Network Against Hate Online (INACH) in Brussels on Monday that Twitter has revised its terms of service comprehensively to promote compliance with European law.

"The rules have been modified to allow us to remove more content when specific groups are targeted," Cartes Andres told JTA. She added that before the lawsuit, "if you weren't targeting anyone, you might have gotten away with it."

This change took effect five months ago, but Twitter "wants to keep a low profile about it because there are other groups, such as free speech groups, who are very unhappy about it, because they see it as censorship," Cartes Andres said.

INACH Secretary Suzette Bronkhorst stated that Twitter's adjusted policy is part of a "shift in attitude" by online service providers since 2013.

"Before the trial, Twitter gave the finger to Europe," Bronkhorst said. "But they realized that if they wanted to do business in Europe, they needed to comply with European law, and others realized that too."

According to Komaitis, the Twitter case is based on a landmark court ruling from 2000 that forced the search engine Yahoo! to stop selling Nazi

我读过，挺有趣的，但真的没法比。你绝对能感觉到每章结尾营造悬念的节奏，比如“请在下一章揭晓！”之类的，挺有魅力的。但我觉得如果比起海量的中国诗词或许多伟大的英文长诗更喜欢这个，就有点傻。别误会，它是电影级的神作，但它还没达到像《神曲》那样具有惊人质量的高度。

1792

不，18世纪。因为作者在完稿前就死了，导致书在不同作者之间传递，朋友们在闲暇时又添加了额外的章节，这正是书里明确发生的情况。在中国，在大部分历史时期，人们并不尊重小说或长篇散文，哲学著作和论文除外。原因是长篇散文被认为比诗词幼稚且缺乏技巧，而成年人经常玩诗词，官僚也被要求在诗词方面有一定造诣。这使得散文唯一的优势在于它能产生的幻想，但即便如此，诗歌也能做到。

总的来说，这种对人类角色的兴趣在中国并不常见，因为它既不像写一篇关于心理学的论文那样深刻，也不像一系列诗歌那样迅速地展现美感。说实话，中国一直对诗集更感兴趣。

1792

No, the 18th century. Because the author died before finishing, the book was passed between different authors, and friends added extra chapters in their leisure time, which is explicitly stated in the book. In China, for most of its history, people did not respect novels or long prose, except for philosophical works and papers. The reason is that long prose was considered immature and lacking in skill compared to poetry, and adults were often involved in poetry, and officials were also required to have some knowledge of poetry. This made the only advantage of prose the fantasy it could generate, but even poetry could achieve that.

Overall, this interest in human characters is not common in China because it is neither as profound as writing a psychology paper nor as quickly beautiful as a series of poems. To be honest, China has always been more interested in poetry collections.

我可以给你一个超赞的豌豆汤食谱？

希望能给我，我家里可能已经有这些材料了。

1.5升自来水

300克裂豌豆

2块肩肉排

1片干月桂叶

1个根芹

2根韭葱

25克新鲜芹菜

1根烟熏香肠

1

在汤锅中，将水、豌豆、猪排和月桂叶煮沸。盖上锅盖，用小火炖20分钟。

2

与此同时，将根芹去皮并切成2厘米的小块。洗净韭葱并切成半圆环状。将芹菜切成细丝。

3

将肉排从锅中取出并切成小方块。放回锅中。加入根芹、韭葱和四分之三的芹菜并搅拌。将烟熏香肠放入汤中，继续炖20分钟，直到所有蔬菜煮熟。经常搅拌。用土豆压泥器将汤（不要压香肠！）压几下。

4

将烟熏香肠从锅中取出，切片并搅拌进汤里。用剩下的芹菜装饰。

>>19236114 (OP)

Drink soup, black person. I had two wisdom teeth pulled at once, couldn't eat anything for a whole week, lol. But they gave me some strong painkillers, so it was pretty nice.

Can I give you a super awesome pea soup recipe?

Hope you do, I might have these ingredients at home.

1.5 liters of tap water

300g split peas

2 pork shoulder chops

1 dried bay leaf

1 root celery

2 leeks

25g fresh celery

1 smoked sausage

1

In a pot, bring the water, peas, pork chops, and bay leaf to a boil. Cover and simmer on low heat for 20 minutes.

2

Meanwhile, peel and cut the root celery into 2cm pieces. Wash the leeks and cut them into half-moon rings. Shred the celery.

3

Remove the pork chops from the pot and cut them into small cubes. Return them to the pot. Add the root celery, leeks, and three-quarters of the celery and stir. Add the smoked sausage to the soup and continue to simmer for 20 minutes until all vegetables are cooked. Stir often. Mash the soup (don't mash the sausage!) a few times with a potato masher.

4

Remove the smoked sausage from the pot, slice it, and stir it into the soup. Garnish with the remaining celery.

This guy is awesome

>>19236114 (OP)

I went through it last month. I made a high-collagen beef bone broth, then cooked noodles and vegetables in it, added some ground beef until everything was soft, then let it cool to room temperature, and sucked/drank it from the side of my mouth farthest from the wound while tilting my head. The blood clot fell off after 4 days, and the hole healed completely without blood (no dry socket), and by the 7th day I could eat normally.

So enjoy the week of soup, make sure you eat high-collagen, high-protein soup, and supplement with zinc!! It speeds up healing!

text:
位于埃多州贝宁的一家联邦高等法院已判处 Favour Eboigbe 10年监禁，原因是其将六名女孩贩卖到布基纳法索从事卖淫活动。

Eboigbe 在 A. A. Okeke 法官面前接受了12项指控的审理。

据了解，这名37岁的男子是在收到举报后，于2009年被埃多州国家禁止人口贩运机构（NAPTIP）的官员在国外抓获的。

受害者年龄在15岁至21岁之间，在被救出后被送往 NAPTIP。

Eboigbe 对指控表示不认罪，这些指控被认为违反了2003年《人口贩运（禁止）执法与管理法》第15a、16和19b条。

然而，在2016年2月29日案件恢复审理时，Okeke 法官判处其10年监禁。

与此同时，NAPTIP 贝宁分区司令部表示，自2004年以来，已在埃多州成功营救了1200名女孩，并获得了55次定罪。

该司令部的发言人 Nduka Nwanwene 先生表示，赋予该机构权力的法律已经得到加强，以防止尼日利亚人被不法分子运往国外进行性剥削。
translation:
A federal high court in Benin, Edo State, has sentenced Favour Eboigbe to 10 years in prison for trafficking six girls to Burkina Faso for prostitution.

Eboigbe faced 12 charges before Judge A. A. Okeke.

It is understood that the 37-year-old man was apprehended abroad in 2009 by officials from the National Agency for the Prohibition of Trafficking in Persons (NAPTIP) in Edo State after receiving a tip-off.

The victims were aged between 15 and 21 and were sent to NAPTIP after being rescued.

Eboigbe pleaded not guilty to the charges, which are believed to violate sections 15a, 16, and 19b of the Trafficking in Persons (Prohibition) Enforcement and Administration Act of 2003.

However, when the case was resumed on February 29, 2016, Judge Okeke sentenced him to 10 years in prison.

Meanwhile, the NAPTIP Benin Zonal Command stated that since 2004, it has successfully rescued 1200 girls in Edo State and secured 55 convictions.

Mr. Nduka Nwanwene, a spokesperson for the command, said that the laws empowering the agency have been strengthened to prevent Nigerians from being transported abroad by criminals for sexual exploitation.

text:
我习惯在每个季节都制定宏大的计划。我是一个渴望生活中有改变和律动的古怪生物，所以对我来说，与其说是每年一次的新开始，我更像是一个每个季节都要重启的女孩。话虽如此，这个夏天的计划是“活在当下”。很简单，对吧？其实没那么简单。

自从我开始尝试神圣瑜伽（Holy Yoga）以来，我在这一领域有了飞跃式的进步，但仍有改进空间。我的计划是停用一个月社交媒体，但结果变成了停用一个月Facebook（我也尝试停用了两周Instagram，但那里的成瘾程度没那么强）。Facebook，那个在我很多年前刚开始用时看起来如此美好的东西，现在已经成长为一个吸干我灵魂的怪物。好吧，这可能有点戏剧化，但对于像我这样心软的共情者来说，看到某些东西真的会让我感到恶心，但我还是会继续阅读那些充满恨意、伤人的文字。或者很多相当无聊的东西，但显然我的生活很无聊，因为我仍然允许自己的时间被这种毫无意义的活动占据。言归正传。活在当下。Nicole，你到底在说什么？我说的是沉浸在此时此刻。承认每一次经历，无论多么微小，都至关重要。所以，这里是我的一些发现。我把它们限制在5条，但关于这个话题我可以写很——长——一段时间。

1. 我热爱午睡！我的目标是每天睡一次，没错，我不觉得这有什么可丢人的。我也推荐大家这么做。我认为学校和每份工作都应该强制要求午睡。把手机放在另一个房间，关掉电视，离开电脑，试一试。我投票支持我们每个人每天都睡个午觉！！！

2. 当你对进食感到兴奋、细细品味每一口时，食物才是最美味的。导致美国人肥胖的不是美食，而是无意识的进食。我在自我说教。

3. 意大利女性的身材极其健美。我这个月的一部分时间在欧洲度过。这些女性的健美程度简直不可思议，但我竟然没看到一家健身房，瑜伽馆也没看到超过一家。她们每天喝卡布奇诺和葡萄酒，吃意面和冰淇淋。我不知道是怎么回事，也许是基因更好，但我很想尝试住在意大利来验证这个理论，哈哈。我的猜测是因为第2点，她们在用餐体验中确实更活在当下。必须采纳这一点。

4. 做爱应该是每天的一部分！！！你的眼睛是不是瞪大了？抱歉。我其实不只是在说发生性关系。我是说对你的伴侣“活在当下”。了解什么会让他们受伤，什么能治愈他们，每个人都不同。如果你在乎，你会学习他们个体的爱之语并用这种方式与他们交流。我对以一种激进的方式爱他人这个想法有点痴迷，但如果我不能对我的丈夫活在当下，那我为什么要尝试对其他人这样做。他是第一个需要知道我对他的爱有多深的人！

5. 最后也是最重要的一点……对于那些认为这些事情不够精神化，以为我只信耶稣、上帝之类的人，事实是这样的：这一切都是精神性的！每一件事。午睡。进食。旅行。爱。尤其是爱。我记得年轻时制定优先级清单，总是确保上帝在最顶端，并且经常感到愧疚，因为我从未“做得足够多”来证明这一点。后来我意识到，祂不在顶端，因为我的清单不是一份清单，而是一段生活，而祂是中心，辐射到我的每一个方面。祂在我所做的一切和我的每一个存在部分之中。这让事情变得有些不同。让活在祂的临在之中成为了我存在的意义，这比努力去“成为”某种样子要轻松得多，我只是祂的:)

这就是我对夏天，或者至少是对七月的宏大总结。所以这让我想要远离Facebook，但我没有，今天又跳回去了，但希望我的体验会有所不同。如果我能有意识地活在当下，我也能有意识地限制我允许像FB这样愚蠢的东西所占据的时间。
translation:
I have a habit of making grand plans for every season. I am a quirky creature who craves change and rhythm in life, so rather than a new beginning every year, I'm more like a girl who restarts every season. That said, this summer's plan is "living in the moment." Simple, right? It's not that simple.

Since I started trying Holy Yoga, I've made leaps and bounds in this area, but there's still room for improvement. My plan was to quit social media for a month, but it turned into quitting Facebook for a month (I also tried quitting Instagram for two weeks, but the addiction there isn't as strong). Facebook, something that looked so wonderful when I first started using it years ago, has now grown into a monster that drains my soul. Okay, that might be a bit dramatic, but for an empathetic person like me, seeing certain things genuinely makes me sick, yet I still keep reading those hateful, hurtful words. Or a lot of boring stuff, but clearly my life is boring because I still allow my time to be consumed by these meaningless activities. Back to the point. Living in the moment. Nicole, what are you even talking about? I mean immersing myself in the present moment. Acknowledging every experience, no matter how small, is crucial. So, here are some of my findings. I'm limiting them to 5, but I could write about this topic for a very—long—time.

1. I love naps! My goal is to nap once a day, yes, I don't think there's anything shameful about it. I recommend it to everyone. I think schools and every workplace should mandate naps. Put your phone in another room, turn off the TV, step away from the computer, just try it. I vote for every one of us to take a nap every day!!!

2. Food is the most delicious when you are excited about eating it, savoring every bite. What makes Americans obese isn't gourmet food, it's mindless eating. I'm preaching to myself.

3. Italian women have incredibly fit bodies. I spent part of this month in Europe. The level of fitness these women have is unbelievable, yet I couldn't find a single gym, and there wasn't more than one yoga studio. They drink cappuccino and wine every day, eat pasta and ice cream. I don't know what it is, maybe their genes are better, but I really want to try living in Italy to test this theory, haha. My guess is it's because of point 2; they are truly more present in their dining experience. This must be adopted.

4. Sex should be a part of every day!!! Are your eyes wide open? Sorry. I don't just mean having sexual relations. I mean being "present in the moment" with your partner. Understanding what hurts them and what heals them—everyone is different. If you care, you will learn their individual love languages and communicate with them in that way. I'm a bit obsessed with the idea of loving others in a radical way, but if I can't be present in the moment with my husband, why would I try to do it with anyone else? He's the first person who needs to know how deeply I love him!

5. Last but not least... for those who think these things aren't spiritual enough, who think I only believe in Jesus, God, or something, here's the truth: all of this is spiritual! Everything. Napping. Eating. Traveling. Loving. Especially loving. I remember when I was young, making priority lists, always ensuring God was at the very top, and often feeling guilty because I never "did enough" to prove it. Later I realized, He isn't at the top because my list wasn't a list, but a life, and He is the center, radiating into every aspect of me. He is in everything I do and every part of my being. This changes things a bit. Making living in His presence the meaning of my existence is much easier than trying to "become" some kind of person; I am just Him's :).

This is my grand summary for summer, or at least for July. So this makes me want to stay away from Facebook, but I didn't, I jumped back on today, but I hope my experience will be different. If I can consciously live in the moment, I can also consciously limit the time I allow stupid things like FB to take up.

text:
在全球基督徒殉道的背景下……如此多的人在聚集敬拜和祷告时被杀害：
在生命的任何阶段，请永远记住，没有任何人，任何环境，任何政府，任何充满恨意、被魔鬼附身的恐怖分子（无论在国内还是国外），能够夺走我们祷告的自由，以及相信耶稣基督是以肉身降临的神的自由。祂舍弃了自己的生命，为了赐予我们怜悯、自由、解放以及克服任何罪孽、软弱或困难的恩典！祂赐予我们恩典叠恩典，让我们原谅那些迫害我们的人，即使是在面对殉道之时！
我称之为“死亡”的恩典！当耶稣在十字架上为我们的罪而死时，祂为我们所有人树立了榜样：“父啊，赦免他们，因为他们不知道自己在做什么！”当圣斯蒂芬被用石头砸死时，他仍在为那些杀死他的人祷告！
使徒行传 7:59-60 “他们在用石头打他的时候，斯蒂芬祷告说：‘主耶稣，接收我的灵魂。’他跪下，大声喊道：‘主啊，不要将这个罪归在他们账上！’说完，他就死去了。”
24/7/365 >>> 耶稣基督就是以马内利，神与我们同在！愿神用悔改的洗礼祝福我们的国家，从那些奉祂名而称呼的人开始。我祈祷圣洁的浪潮和对主的敬畏将席卷全球，带来一波又一波浪子回头的人，包括儿子和女儿，丈夫和妻子，祭司和先知。奉耶稣之名，阿门，阿门。主啊，就如此吧。
约翰福音 15:11-15……“我对你们说这些话，是要叫我的喜乐在你们心里，使你们的喜乐可以满足。我的命令就是：你们要像我爱你们一样，彼此相爱。没有比愿意为自己的朋友舍命更大的爱了。你们若遵我的命令行，就是我的朋友……现在，我把父告诉我的事都告诉你们，所以你们成了我的朋友。”
肩并肩，恩典叠恩典，Betty Jordan
translation:
In the context of Christian martyrdom around the world... so many people have been killed while gathering to worship and pray:
At any stage of life, always remember that no one, no environment, no government, no terrorist filled with hatred and possessed by the devil (whether at home or abroad), can take away our freedom to pray, or the freedom to believe in Jesus Christ, who came in the flesh. He gave up His life to grant us mercy, freedom, liberation, and the grace to overcome any sin, weakness, or difficulty! He gives us grace upon grace, enabling us to forgive those who persecute us, even when facing martyrdom!
I call it the grace of "death"! When Jesus died on the cross for our sins, He set an example for all of us: "Father, forgive them, for they do not know what they are doing!" When Stephen was stoned to death, he was still praying for those who killed him!
Acts 7:59-60 "While they were stoning him, Stephen prayed, 'Lord Jesus, receive my spirit.' He fell to his knees and cried out with a loud voice, 'Lord, do not hold this sin against them!'"
24/7/365 >>> Jesus Christ is Emmanuel, God is with us! May God bless our nation with the baptism of repentance, starting with those who call upon Him in His name. I pray that a wave of holiness and reverence for the Lord will sweep across the globe, bringing wave after wave of people to turn back, including sons and daughters, husbands and wives, priests and prophets. In Jesus' name, Amen, Amen. Lord, so be it.
John 15:11-15... "I have told you these things so that my joy may be in you and that your joy may be complete. My command is this: Love each other as I have loved you. Greater love has no one than this: to lay down one's life for one's friends. If you keep my commands, you are my friends... Now I have told you what my Father has told me, so that you may be my friends."
Shoulder to shoulder, grace upon grace, Betty Jordan
